hook: You already know how to count and work with everyday numbers like 5, 12, or 100. But what happens when a temperature drops *below* zero, or your bank account goes negative? In this unit, you'll unlock a whole new side of the number system that makes sense of situations where numbers go in both directions.
concept: Every topic in this unit builds on one powerful idea: numbers have both a size and a direction, and once you understand that, you can add, subtract, multiply, and divide across the entire integer number line — formally known as the set of integers $\mathbb{Z}$.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to recognize and interpret positive and negative numbers as quantities with opposite meanings, like earning $10 versus owing $10.
Master using the number line as a visual tool to locate, read, and reason about integers on both sides of zero.
You will learn to find the absolute value of any integer, understanding it as a number's distance from zero regardless of direction, written as $|{-7}| = 7$.
Apply your number line skills to correctly compare integers using $<$, $>$, and $=$, and arrange any set of integers from least to greatest.
Master the rules for adding and subtracting positive and negative integers so you can confidently evaluate expressions like $-8 + 3$ or $5 - (-2)$.
You will learn how the signs of two integers determine whether their product or quotient is positive or negative, turning tricky problems like $(-4) 	imes (-6)$ into straightforward calculations.
Apply all four integer operations together by following the correct order of operations to evaluate multi-step expressions like $-3 + 2 	imes (4 - 7)^2$ accurately.
